name: SCC_CreateChargingStatusRes_ISO
title: SCC_CreateChargingStatusRes_ISO
syntax: long SCC_CreateChargingStatusRes_ISO ( byte SessionID[], char ResponseCode[], long NotificationMaxDelay, byte StatusFlags[], char Notification[], char EVSEID[], long SAScheduleTupleId )
description: Creates a Charging Status Response message for sending.

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: 8-byte long SessionID of SCC connection, range: 0 - 0xFF FF FF FF FF FF FF FF.

parameters.name: NotificationMaxDelay
parameters.description: Time until the vehicle is expected to react on the notification (for AC_EVSEStatus).

parameters.name: StatusFlags
parameters.description: Flags for AC_EVSEStatus: StatusFlags[0] = PowerSwitchClosed StatusFlags[1] = RCD.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: Notification about an action that the charge point wants the vehicle to perform (for AC_EVSEStatus).

parameters.name: Caution! EVSEID is interpreted as a 64 bit number, and thus must not contain any letters.
SAScheduleTupleId
parameters.description: Unique ID of a SAScheduleTuple which identifies the selected Tariff.
MeterID char[] Unique ID of the meter.
MeterReading long Current meter reading in Wh.
SigMeterReading byte[] Signature of the meter reading (length 64).
MeterStatus long Current status of the meter.
TMeter long Timestamp of the current SECC time.
ReceiptRequired long Indicates if the vehicle is required to sent a MeteringReceiptReq.

parameters.name: Caution! Due to the requirements of the schema, the element “MeterID” is mandatory if any of the optional meter-related parameters are to be supplied.

return:
Time until the vehicle is expected to react 
on the
availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
